action_description: Announced Amador Energy Storage Project (100MW/200MWh BESS) approximately 11 miles southwest of Canton, Van Zandt County, TX
Presented Amador BESS project to Van Zandt County Commissioners Court at workshop meeting

entity: Ronald Lane, Nancy White, and 18 other Van Zandt County residents
action_description: Filed lawsuit (Cause No. 24-00204) seeking damages, temporary and permanent injunction against BT Amador Storage LLC, Maria Amador, Jose Valedelmar Amador, RES Group LLC, Belltown Power Texas Land 2 LLC, and CSC Trust Company of Delaware
legal_mechanism_raw: Petition for Damages, Temporary and Permanent Injunction and Jury Demand — 294th Judicial District Court, Van Zandt County

entity: Van Zandt County residents (100+ in attendance)
action_description: Public meeting held where residents voiced safety concerns including risk of fire and environmental damage from lithium battery explosion

entity: Van Zandt County Fire Marshal (Kevin Palmer)
action_description: Issued order to Taaleri Energia to conform to all applicable NFPA standards per Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233 and NFPA 855 (2020 ed). Required: permit application, UL container test results, site survey, hazard mitigation plan, emergency response plan, permanent water source documentation. Compliance deadline: April 7, 2025
legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Local Government Code Chapter 233; NFPA 855 (2020 ed)

entity: Van Zandt County District Attorney (Tonda Curry) / 294th District Court Judge Chris Martin
action_description: DA presented ex parte petition for Temporary Restraining Order. Judge Martin signed TRO (Cause No. 25-00067) against Taaleri Energia North America LLC, BT Amador Storage LLC, Amador BESS Holdings Inc., Renewable Energy Systems Americas Inc., and Belltown Texas Power 2 LLC. TRO in place for 14 days, prohibiting batteries from entering the county until NFPA compliance achieved
legal_mechanism_raw: Temporary Restraining Order — 294th Judicial District Court, Van Zandt County; Texas Health & Safety Code

action_description: Court hearing scheduled for July 24, 2025 to determine whether TRO will be extended or batteries allowed in

entity: Van Zandt County officials (Fire Marshal Kevin Palmer, DA Tonda Curry, Commissioner Cliff Williams)
action_description: Confirmed TRO still in effect. No compliant safety test results received from developer. Groundwork continues but no batteries permitted in county
Unanimously adopted temporary moratorium freezing all proposed green energy developments (solar farms, wind installations, BESS) in unincorporated areas. Passed after hours of public testimony
legal_mechanism_raw: Texas Health & Safety Code §121.003 (county police powers for health, safety, and welfare)

action_description: Passed resolution opposing Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in Gillespie County, citing safety, economic, and environmental concerns. Also passed resolution requiring documentation for Harper BESS project
As of this date, Peregrine Energy Solutions had not filed any of the required construction documents with Gillespie County. LCRA connection construction continued.

entity: Victor Hollender, Bruce Neitzke, Esther Schneider, Sarah Rivas (Harper residents)
action_description: Filed lawsuit in 216th Judicial District Court against Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC and B&CWR Inc. (d/b/a Cactus Construction), seeking temporary and permanent injunction to stop construction of 145MW BESS due to fire risk and chemical contamination concerns, proximity to Harper ISD (~1,700 feet)
legal_mechanism_raw: Petition for Temporary and Permanent Injunction — 216th Judicial District Court, Gillespie County

action_description: Filed Petition in Intervention in 216th Judicial District Court, joining the residents' lawsuit as a plaintiff opposing the Rogers Draw BESS. Simultaneously filed Response in Opposition to Rogers Draw's Rule 91a Motion to Dismiss
legal_mechanism_raw: Petition in Intervention — 216th Judicial District Court, Gillespie County (Cause No. 25-18363)

action_description: Voted to take action on two energy projects. County announced it would file petition of intervention against Peregrine Energy (confirmed the Nov 26 filing). Residents packed meeting to voice concerns. Evidence of new LCRA utility pole construction found at Harper site
Issued Stop Work Order to Rogers Draw Energy Storage LLC for the Rogers Draw BESS project in Harper, Texas

Filed countersuit against Gillespie County in 216th Judicial District Court, claiming the county had initially approved the project and reversed course after citizen pressure. Sought dismissal of county's intervention

entity: 216th District Court Judge Albert Patillo / All parties
action_description: Agreed temporary injunction signed halting all construction activities on the Rogers Draw BESS project at Harper until final judgment. Trial date to be set
legal_mechanism_raw: Agreed Temporary Injunction — 216th Judicial District Court

entity: Vesper Energy / Ochoa Energy Storage LLC
action_description: Applied for Special Use Permit (SUP) to build Ochoa Energy Storage (500MW BESS) on approximately 24-acre property at 27051 Highway Blvd, Katy, TX. Holds two ERCOT queue positions (Ochoa I & II) representing 750MW cumulative capacity
legal_mechanism_raw: Special Use Permit application — City of Katy zoning ordinance

entity: City of Katy Planning and Zoning Committee
action_description: Recommended approval of the Special Use Permit for Ochoa Energy Storage

entity: Vesper Energy (Community Affairs Manager Alex Rohr)
action_description: Held "lunch and learn" community session for local residents to provide details on Ochoa project and energy storage industry

Unanimously denied Special Use Permit for Ochoa Energy Storage after public hearing with multiple residents voicing opposition. Overrode Planning and Zoning Committee recommendation. Motion by Councilmember Dan Smith, seconded by Councilmember Janet Corte. Concerns cited: fire safety, proximity to Katy High School and Katy ISD Outdoor Learning Center, residential areas, Falcon Point Apartments
legal_mechanism_raw: Denial of Special Use Permit — City of Katy zoning authority

action_description: Citizens expressed continued opposition at city council meeting. Mayor Pro Tem Chris Harris cited proximity to schools and residential areas

Filed appeal and application for declaratory order with Texas Public Utility Commission (PUC Docket No. 58859, SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508). Argued City of Katy's zoning actions exceed regulatory authority under Public Utility Regulatory Act (PURA). Requested PUC declare city ordinances "null and void." Moved for immediate referral to State Office of Administrative Hearings (SOAH)
legal_mechanism_raw: Appeal and Application for Declaratory Order — Texas PUC; PURA §39.001(c)(d), §39.151

action_description: Filed response to Ochoa's PUC appeal, arguing city ordinances do not interfere with PUC or ERCOT jurisdiction and do not conflict with state authority under PURA
legal_mechanism_raw: Response to Appeal — Texas PUC Docket No. 58859

action_description: Filed with PUC stating direct interest in the outcome of the case, asserting city's denial affects grid authority. PUC decision delayed
legal_mechanism_raw: Intervention / Statement of Interest — Texas PUC Docket No. 58859

action_description: Procedural schedule set: Ochoa direct testimony April 10, 2026; City direct testimony May 15, 2026; Staff and ERCOT testimony June 1, 2026; Cross-rebuttal July 6, 2026; Hearing on the Merits August 4-5, 2026
legal_mechanism_raw: Procedural Schedule — PUC Docket No. 58859 / SOAH Docket No. 473-26-07508

action_description: Proposed Marshall Springs Solar Complex and Battery Energy Storage System (~1,100 acres, 150MW solar + 150MW BESS) near Willow City in Gillespie County, TX. AMPYR described as an Indonesian energy investment company with no history of completed projects in Texas or the US
Communicated in writing to AMPYR Energy that the Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance applies to the proposed Marshall Springs project
legal_mechanism_raw: Written notice — Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance

action_description: Passed resolution opposing Solar and BESS projects, specifically targeting the proposed Marshall Springs project. Required Marshall Springs Solar Farm and BESS documentation

Sent letter to AMPYR Energy pressing for answers regarding the proposed 1,000-acre BESS and solar facility, citing concerns about Hill Country landscape, drought, wildfire, flash flooding risk, volunteer fire department capacity, and proximity to rural residences
Sent formal Notice of Applicability of Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance to the proposed Marshall Springs Solar Complex and Battery Energy Storage System Project
legal_mechanism_raw: Formal notice — Gillespie County Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance

entity: Gillespie County Commissioners Court / Gillespie County residents
action_description: Residents packed commissioners court meeting to voice continued concerns with both the Marshall Springs and Rogers Draw projects

Held information meeting with senior representatives of federal, state, county, and city officials. Attendees included aides to U.S. Senator John Cornyn, U.S. Senator Ted Cruz, U.S. Rep. Chip Roy, Texas Senator Pete Flores, and Texas Representative Ellen Troxclair
legal_mechanism_raw: Citizen-organized informational meeting with elected officials

action_description: Signed ERCOT interconnection agreement for 150MW Marshall Springs Solar project in Gillespie County, advancing the development despite local opposition
Sent follow-up letter to AMPYR Energy renewing pressure, requesting answers on Chinese components and funding sources, environmental risks, and community impact
Filed lawsuit (Case No. 26-18599) against Marshall Springs Storage LLC, Marshall Springs Solar LLC, and Ampyr Energy USA LLC in Gillespie County District Court
legal_mechanism_raw: Lawsuit filing — Gillespie County District Court
